name: Pretzels for the Breadmaker
recipeInstructions:
Add ingredients into the bread machine in this order. Set on Manual.
1. FORMING: Cut dough into short strips, roll into ropes and shape into pretzels. Cover with a tea towel and let rise for 45 minutes.
2. 'BATH': In a cast iron or other non-aluminum pan, bring almost to a boil 4 cups of water and 5 teaspoons of baking soda. Gently place the pretzels (by hand or using a slotted spoon) into the water for approximately 15 seconds on each side, turning once. Do not let the water come to a boil. Remove pretzels and place on a greased baking sheet. Sprinkle with coarse salt (kosher).
3. Bake at 475°F for 12 minutes. Note: In my oven it took only about 10 minutes, so watch carefully. Do not make the strips too fat; they really puff up. If you are on a salt-free diet, use sesame, poppy, or garlic sprinkles on the outside...the whole family loved them and the DAK did all the hard work.
If your machine requires the water first, add ingredients in reverse order: from Donna German's THE BREAD MACHINE COOKBOOK I and Grandma Elaine's kitchen.
